reasoning: The question asks whether there will be exactly 5 rate cuts (totalling 1.25% or 125 bps) by the Fed in 2025. As of April 2025, the Fed has held rates steady at both the January and March FOMC meetings, keeping the target rate at 4.25%-4.50%. There are only 6 scheduled meetings remaining in the year. The March 2025 Summary of Economic Projections ("dot plot") indicates a median expectation of only 2 rate cuts (50 bps) in 2025. For the market to resolve "Yes", the Fed must cut exactly 5 times (or 125 bps) across the remaining 6 meetings. This would require an aggressive easing cycle that avoids going beyond 125 bps (which would trigger a "No" resolution for 6+ cuts). Given current macroeconomic projections of stronger growth and sticky inflation, this outcome is highly improbable. The Polymarket price on April 3 was 0.05, which perfectly reflects this narrow tail probability. I will forecast at the lower bound limit of 0.05.
